title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria pop-up bar in Seaport celebrates women's sports, opened March 19, and available until April 30.
The bar honors Kathrine Switzer's Boston Marathon legacy with themed menu items like Switzer's Sliders and Kathrine Spritzer.
A portion of cocktail sales supports Dream Big!, aiding girls from low-income households in sports participation.

title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to repay $224,063 for misusing nonprofit funds and fraudulently obtained pandemic relief.
Cannon-Grant, former head of Violence in Boston, pleaded guilty to fraud and sentenced to home confinement and probation.
Judge set restitution at $106,003, with total forfeiture including diverted donations and fraudulent benefits.

title: India-based Tanishq jewelry opens its first store in New England
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Tanishq jewelry opens its first New England store at 303 Turnpike Road, Westborough.
The 4,000-square-foot showroom in Westborough is Tanishq's 10th U.S. location.
Tanishq is expanding in the U.S. to cater to the growing Indian diaspora, particularly in New England.

title: NH athlete is running Boston for the care that keeps her breathing
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Alison Clode from Portsmouth, NH, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for medical research.
Clode has been a patient at Mass Eye and Ear for over 10 years, dealing with subglottic stenosis.
Dr. Song's Otolaryngology unit at Mass Eye and Ear aids individuals with conditions affecting breathing.

title: A major North Shore office park is adding 60 new condos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cummings Properties is adding 60 new condos to the Cumming Center in Beverly, targeting 55-plus buyers looking to downsize.
The development, Beverly Landing, reserves 80% of units for households with at least one resident aged 55 or older.
The new building offers units from 700 to 2,600 square feet, with prices ranging from $500,000 to $1.5 million.

title: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Connelly Early will start in the Red Sox rotation for the 2026 season, beginning with the third game against the Reds.
Early, a 23-year-old left-hander, tied a Red Sox strikeout record in his MLB debut with 11 strikeouts in September 2025.
Johan Oviedo will move to the bullpen following his offseason acquisition, with a reevaluation planned after a few rotation turns.

title: Experience an ‘unforgettable’ Easter weekend trip in this Maine city, according to Booking.com
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Booking.com lists Portland, Maine as one of 15 unforgettable Easter weekend trips in the U.S. for 2026.
Travelers can explore Portland's waterfront, historic Old Port, and enjoy seafood dinners and lighthouse views.
Portland hotels with Michelin keys and James Beard semifinalist restaurants offer accommodations and dining options.

title: Netflix star Emma Hernan is opening a frozen food facility in Hingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emma Hernan is opening a $4.6M frozen food manufacturing facility in Hingham for her brand, Coast to Coast Kitchen.
The 28,000-square-foot facility aims to start production in July, creating jobs for about 50 people.
Hernan's company will produce items like crab cakes, and the factory plans to prioritize hiring local women.

title: This mental health worker is running Boston for her neighbors in South Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sarah Clark, a mental health worker, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for South Boston Neighborhood House.
Clark's work focuses on youth mental health services in Boston and surrounding areas, including Randolph and Quincy.
The South Boston Neighborhood House, known as "The Ollie," offers community support such as education and job assistance.

title: Giveaway: Enter to win tickets to ‘The Drama’
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Enter to win tickets to 'The Drama' by emailing a relationship question to Love Letters from March 24-27.
The screening of 'The Drama' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson will be held Sunday in Somerville.
Participants must be 18 or older, with winners notified via email by 5 p.m. on Friday.
